title: chamaemelum segetum
intro: Chamaemelum segetum, also known as false chamomile or pineapple weed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family and is commonly found in lawns, meadows, and along roadsides. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce anxiety. The health benefits of Chamaemelum segetum include its ability to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Chamaemelum segetum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, which can be used to make herbal remedies for various health conditions.
